hypothesis.H0: P-creation does NOT predict subsequent domain expansion (lift <= 1.0x)
hypothesis.direction: REVERSE (P-creation at t -> domain seeding at t+1..t+w)

method: P-creation sessions identified by cross-referencing S351 experiment's verified list (n=38, through S351) with post-S351 SESSION-LOG.md parsing. Sub-session aggregation (S57a+S57b -> S57) prevents dedup-induced P-loss. Domain-seeding sessions identified via three signals: (1) first/second DOMEX visit per domain from session log, (2) DOMAIN.md file creation from git diff-filter=A, (3) session log domain-expert/seeded mentions. For each P-creation session at time t, checked whether any session in [t+1, t+window] was a domain-seeding session (FORWARD-looking only). Computed lift = P(domain-seed after P-creation) / P(domain-seed | baseline). Fisher exact test (one-sided) for statistical significance.
verdict_detail: Lift > 1.0 (1.135x) but not statistically significant (p=0.422494). Era-controlled analysis reveals strong confound: Early era (24 P-sessions, 0 domain seeds), Late era (3 P-sessions, 21 domain seeds), Mid era drives all overlap. Cannot distinguish temporal co-location from causal link.

prior_experiments.S398_reverse_pcreation: 1.135x lift (P-creation at t -> domain seed at t+1, window=1)

findings: Reverse-direction lift at window=1: 1.135x (7/39 P-creation sessions followed by domain seeding vs 0.158 baseline rate)
Window=2 lift: 1.176x, Window=3 lift: 1.058x, Window=5 lift: 0.947x
P-creation session count: 39, Domain-seed session count: 36, Total sessions: 196

ERA CONFOUND: P-creation concentrated in Early era (S57-S100, 24/39 sessions) where 0 domain seeds exist. Domain seeding concentrated in Mid+Late where P-creation is sparse. The 1.135x lift is driven entirely by the S176-S189 overlap window.
Early era (S57-S100): 24 P-creation sessions, 0 domain seeds. These sessions occurred before the DOMEX protocol existed.

Mid era (S101-S200): 12 P-creation sessions, 13 domain seeds. 7/12 P-sessions followed by domain seeding at t+1 — but this reflects temporal co-location of two independent processes (P-production era ending, domain-seeding era beginning).
Late era (S201-S400): 3 P-creation sessions, 21 domain seeds. P-creation nearly extinct. Domain seeding dominates.
interpretation: The reverse direction shows a marginal lift (1.135x, p=0.422494) that is NON-SIGNIFICANT and ERA-CONFOUNDED. P-creation is concentrated in the Early era (S57-S100, 24/39 sessions) where zero domain seeding occurs (DOMEX protocol didn't exist). Domain seeding is concentrated in the Late era (S301+) where P-creation is nearly extinct (3/39 sessions). The only overlap window (S176-S189) shows high lift (4.77x) but this likely reflects the swarm's developmental transition from principle-production phase to domain-exploration phase, not a causal P-creation -> domain-seeding link. The neuroplasticity isomorphism (P-119) appears UNIDIRECTIONAL: domain novelty triggers principle extraction (S326 forward: 3.66x), but principle creation does not reliably predict subsequent domain expansion. This is consistent with the biological analogy: adult neurogenesis (domain seeding) enables new learning (principle extraction), but learning does not directly trigger neurogenesis.
